perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in all respects in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, without giving effect to any conflicts of laws principles.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in all respects in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, without giving effect to any conflicts of laws principles.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the governing law to Delaware could impact consumer protection, as certain rights afforded under New York law might not be available in Delaware. This introduces a legal risk by potentially weakening consumer protections available to New York residents.
perturbation.law_explanation: NY GBL § 349 prohibits deceptive acts and practices in business. By changing the governing law from New York to Delaware, New York residents may find it more difficult or impossible to pursue claims under NY GBL § 349, effectively removing a consumer protection afforded to them under New York law if the deceptive acts occurred or targeted New York consumers.

Next § 349. Deceptive acts and practices unlawful. (a) Deceptive acts or practices in the conduct of any business, trade or commerce or in the furnishing of any service in this state are hereby declared unlawful. (b) Whenever the attorney general shall believe from evidence satisfactory to him that any person, firm, corporation or association or agent or employee thereof has engaged in or is about to engage in any of the acts or practices stated to be unlawful he may bring an action in the name and on behalf of the people of the state of New York to enjoin such unlawful acts or practices and to obtain restitution of any moneys or property obtained directly or indirectly by any such unlawful acts or practices. In such action preliminary relief may be granted under article sixty-three of the civil practice law and rules. (c) Before any violation of this section is sought to be enjoined, the attorney general shall be required to give the person against whom such proceeding is contemplated notice by certified mail and an opportunity to show in writing within five business days after receipt of notice why proceedings should not be instituted against him, unless the attorney general shall find, in any case in which he seeks preliminary relief, that to give such notice and opportunity is not in the public interest. (d) In any such action it shall be a co
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the contract to be governed by Delaware law instead of New York law. The Law Explanation indicates that this change could make it more difficult for New York residents to pursue claims under NY GBL § 349, a consumer protection law in New York, if deceptive acts occurred or targeted New York consumers. Scraped Law Snippet 2 explicitly states, 'Deceptive acts or practices in the conduct of any business, trade or commerce or in the furnishing of any service in this state are hereby declared unlawful.' By changing the governing law, the contract effectively removes a layer of consumer protection afforded by New York law, creating a legal loophole where New York consumers might not be able to pursue claims under NY GBL § 349.

perturbation.original_text: Any dispute, controversy and/or difference which may arise out of or in connection with or in relation to this Agreement, shall be solely and finally settled by binding arbitration pursuant to then-current rules of the International Chamber of Commerce. Such arbitration shall be held in New York, New York.
perturbation.changed_text: Any dispute, controversy and/or difference which may arise out of or in connection with or in relation to this Agreement, shall be solely and finally settled by binding arbitration pursuant to then-current rules of the International Chamber of Commerce. Such arbitration shall be held in Beijing, China.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the location of arbitration to Beijing, China makes it significantly more difficult for a US-based licensee to effectively pursue disputes, potentially violating consumer protection laws by creating an undue burden.
perturbation.contradicted_law: The FAA (Federal Arbitration Act) and principles of unconscionability under state contract law.
perturbation.law_explanation: The FAA governs arbitration agreements, but courts can refuse to enforce agreements deemed unconscionable. Requiring arbitration in Beijing imposes significant travel and legal costs on the licensee, potentially making it practically impossible to pursue legitimate claims, thus acting as an exculpatory clause and undermining the FAA and state contract law principles of fairness. This is because the distance, cost, and unfamiliar legal system impose an undue burden.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 9 U.S. Code § 2 - Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 9 U.S. Code § 2 - Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate U.S. Code Notes prev | next A written provision in any maritime transaction or a contract evidencing a transaction involving commerce to settle by arbitration a controversy thereafter arising out of such contract or transaction, or the refusal to perform the whole or any part thereof, or an agreement in writing to submit to arbitration an existing controversy arising out of such a contract, transaction, or refusal, shall be valid, irrevocable, and enforceable, save upon such grounds as exist at law or in equity for the revocation of any contract or as otherwise provided in chapter 4. perturbation.justification: The changed text contradicts the principle of fairness and enforceability in arbitration agreements, as interpreted under the FAA and state contract law. By changing the arbitration location to Beijing, China, for a US-based licensee, the agreement imposes significant financial and logistical burdens, effectively hindering their ability to pursue legitimate claims. This undermines the FAA's intent to provide a fair dispute resolution mechanism. 9 U.S. Code § 2 states that arbitration agreements should be "valid, irrevocable, and enforceable, save upon such grounds as exist at law or in equity for the revocation of any contract." Requiring arbitration in a distant and unfamiliar legal jurisdiction could be grounds for revocation based on unconscionability or undue burden.

perturbation.original_text: THE PARTIES SPECIFICALLY WAIVE ANY RIGHT TO TRIAL BY JURY IN ANY COURT WITH RESPECT TO ANY CONTRACTUAL, TORTIOUS, OR STATUTORY CLAIM, COUNTERCLAIM, OR CROSS-CLAIM AGAINST THE OTHER ARISING OUT OF OR CONNECTED IN ANY WAY TO THIS AGREEMENT, BECAUSE THE PARTIES HERETO, BOTH OF WHOM ARE REPRESENTED BY COUNSEL, BELIEVE THAT THE COMPLEX COMMERCIAL AND PROFESSIONAL ASPECTS OF THEIR DEALINGS WITH ONE ANOTHER MAKE A JURY DETERMINATION NEITHER DESIRABLE NOR APPROPRIATE.
perturbation.changed_text: THE PARTIES WAIVE ANY RIGHT TO TRIAL BY JURY IN ANY COURT WITH RESPECT TO ANY CLAIM AGAINST THE OTHER ARISING OUT OF OR CONNECTED IN ANY WAY TO THIS AGREEMENT.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the clause that both parties are represented by counsel creates a legal risk because a jury waiver is more likely to be enforced when both parties are sophisticated and represented by counsel. This omission makes the jury waiver more susceptible to challenge, particularly if one party is not sophisticated or lacks legal representation. This affects the legal rights and protections of the parties.
perturbation.law_explanation: The 7th Amendment guarantees the right to a jury trial in civil cases. While this right can be waived, such waivers must be knowing and voluntary. The original clause explicitly stating both parties are represented by counsel strengthens the argument that the waiver is knowing and voluntary. Removing this statement weakens the waiver's enforceability, as it is a factor considered by courts when evaluating the validity of the waiver. Also, the original text made it clear that the parties found jury determination undesirable because of the complex nature of their dealings, which is also another reason why the agreement can be upheld in court.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Seventh Amendment | U.S. Constitution | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Seventh Amendment The Seventh Amendment continues a practice from English common law of distinguishing civil claims which must be tried before a jury (absent waiver by the parties) from claims and issues that may be heard by a judge alone. It only governs federal civil courts and has no application to civil courts set up by the states when those courts are hearing only disputes of state law . Learn more... Primary tabs Amendment VII In suits at common law, where the value in controversy shall exceed twenty dollars, the right of trial by jury shall be preserved, and no fact tried by a jury, shall be otherwise reexamined in any court of the United States, than according to the rules of the common law. perturbation.justification: The modified text removes a key element that supports the enforceability of the jury waiver. The original text explicitly states that both parties are represented by counsel, strengthening the argument that the jury waiver was knowing and voluntary. The Law Explanation makes it clear that removing this statement weakens the waiver's enforceability. The Perturbation Explanation confirms that the omission of this clause creates a legal risk. Scraped Law Snippet 2 references the Seventh Amendment, which guarantees the right to a jury trial; this right can be waived, however such waiver must be knowing and voluntary. Removing the statement that the parties were represented by counsel introduces an Omission Legal Contradiction as it removes essential information, creating a legal loophole that makes the jury waiver more susceptible to challenge. This falls under the definition of Omission Legal Contradiction.
